author: phase-4-coordinator
content:
Audited ferray-core/src/manipulation/mod.rs against NumPy. concatenate, stack, vstack, hstack, dstack, block, split, array_split, vsplit, hsplit, dsplit are all already implemented. Filled the actual gaps:

1. Array::to_dyn() and Array::into_dyn() in ferray-core/src/array/methods.rs — converts a typed Array<T,D> to Array<T,IxDyn>. Without this, users with statically-typed arrays couldn't even call concatenate (which takes &[Array<T,IxDyn>]).

2. column_stack(arrays) — stacks 1-D arrays as columns of a 2-D array. For 2-D+ inputs, equivalent to hstack. Validates length matches across 1-D inputs.

3. row_stack(arrays) — alias for vstack to match NumPy's row_stack.

4. array_split_n(a, n, axis) — NumPy's array_split with integer sections (allows uneven division). The first (axis_len % n) sections get one extra element. Existing split() requires even division (matches numpy.split); existing array_split() requires explicit indices. This fills the gap for the int-sections variant of array_split.

5. 9 new tests covering: column_stack 1D→2D, column_stack 2D fall-through, length mismatch error, row_stack=vstack equivalence, array_split_n uneven (7÷3), array_split_n even (6÷3), array_split_n more sections than elements (3÷5 → empty trailing parts), to_dyn round-trip, end-to-end concatenate of typed Ix2 arrays via to_dyn().

Result: 75 manipulation tests pass (was 66), workspace tests + clippy clean. Closing as completed.
